clusters.title: Mojtaba Khamenei Named Iran's New Supreme Leader
clusters.summary: Mojtaba Khamenei has been appointed as Iran's new supreme leader, succeeding his father, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, who passed away recently. This transition marks a continuation of the hard-line policies that have characterized Iran's leadership, amid heightened tensions with regional rivals and the West. The appointment is seen as a significant moment for Iran's political landscape, reinforcing the influence of the Khamenei family in the country's governance.

items.title: New Supreme Leader Inherits Sprawling, Secretive Office That Dominates Iran
items.description: His father, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, had turned what was traditionally a religious affairs office into a shadowy national security juggernaut.

clusters.title: Anthropic Files Lawsuit Against US Government Over Risk Designation
clusters.summary: AI company Anthropic has initiated a lawsuit against the US government, specifically targeting the Pentagon and the Trump administration, to challenge a 'supply chain risk' designation that could impact its operations. The designation raises concerns about the company's ability to secure contracts and maintain its business viability. This legal action underscores the ongoing tensions between government regulations and the tech industry, particularly in the rapidly evolving field of artificial intelligence.

clusters.title: Australia Grants Asylum to Five Iranian Women Soccer Players
clusters.summary: Australia has granted asylum to five members of the Iranian women's soccer team, allowing them to remain in the country amid ongoing concerns about their safety. The move has garnered attention in light of international discussions surrounding human rights and sports.

clusters.title: Country Joe McDonald, Woodstock Icon, Passes Away at 84
clusters.summary: Country Joe McDonald, the influential musician known for his anti-war anthem 'I-Feel-Like-I'm-Fixin'-To-Die Rag,' has died at the age of 84. McDonald was a prominent figure in the 1960s counterculture movement and gained fame at the Woodstock festival. His legacy as a voice for peace and protest continues to resonate in American music history.

clusters.title: Donna Ockenden Appointed to Lead Leeds Maternity Services Inquiry
clusters.summary: Donna Ockenden has been appointed to chair an inquiry into maternity services at hospitals in Leeds, following concerns over care quality. Families affected by past incidents have expressed cautious optimism about the appointment, viewing it as a step towards rebuilding trust in the healthcare system. The decision by Health Secretary Wes Streeting aims to address ongoing issues and improve maternal care in the region.

items.title: U-turn by Streeting sees senior midwife Ockenden appointed to chair maternity inquiry
items.description: Donna Ockenden is appointed to lead the maternity services inquiry at Leeds hospitals.

items.title: Families welcome appointment of Donna Ockenden to Leeds maternity inquiry
items.description:
Campaigners say Wes Streeting’s decision to name senior midwife as chair will begin to restore trust

Families who lost babies at two hospitals in Leeds have said they are slowly regaining trust in the health secretary after the midwife Donna Ockenden was appointed to lead a review into the failing service, where 56 babies and two mothers died in five years.

Ockenden, who conducted a similar review into maternity services at Shrewsbury and Telford hospital NHS trust in 2020, was chosen to lead the investigation into Leeds teaching hospitals NHS trust after a campaign by the families.

Continue reading...

clusters.summary: Former President Trump has stated that he would respond to any disruption in oil shipments through the Strait of Hormuz with military action '20 times harder' than previous responses. The situation highlights the strategic importance of the Strait of Hormuz for global oil supply and the potential for escalating conflict.

clusters.title: Missouri Man's Murder Charge Dropped After Super Bowl Shooting
clusters.summary: A Missouri man who opened fire at a rally celebrating the Kansas City Chiefs' Super Bowl victory had his murder charge dropped, with prosecutors citing self-defense laws. The incident involved multiple shooters and occurred outside Union Station, leading to significant public concern. The man has been sentenced to two years in prison for his actions during the event.

items.title: Missouri drops murder charge against man who opened fire at Super Bowl rally
items.description:
Man was among at least six people who started shooting outside Union Station in state, which has adopted stand your ground law

  • Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox

A man who initially faced a murder charge for opening fire following the Kansas City Chiefs’ 2024 Super Bowl win was sentenced Monday to two years in prison in a case prosecutors said was complicated by the state’s self-defense laws.

Dominic Miller, who pleaded guilty to a weapons charge as part of a plea deal, was among at least six people to start shooting in the melee that sent players, city officials and hundreds of fans scrambling for cover, according to court records.

Continue reading...

items.title: Prosecutor drops murder charge against shooter at Chiefs rally and cites self defense laws
items.description: A man who initially faced a murder charge for opening fire after the Kansas City Chiefs’ 2024 Super Bowl win was sentenced Monday to just two years in prison

The move underscores Ukraine's commitment to international defense cooperation amid ongoing regional tensions.

items.title: Ukraine sent drone experts to protect US bases in Jordan, says Zelenskyy
items.description:
Interceptor drones and operators deployed to Middle East after ‘requests for help from 11 countries neighbouring Iran’

  • Middle East crisis – live updates

Ukraine’s president has said he dispatched interceptor drones and operators to protect US bases in Jordan last week, one of 11 countries that had asked Kyiv for help as the US-Israeli war against Iran continued into its 10th day.

Volodymyr Zelenskyy said in an interview that he had responded to a US request for help in defending Jordan last week as Ukraine seeks to improve relations with Gulf and Middle Eastern countries coming under attack from Iran.

Continue reading...

clusters.title: Georgia Special Election to Replace Marjorie Taylor Greene
clusters.summary: Voters in Georgia's congressional district are participating in a special election to select a replacement for Marjorie Taylor Greene, who recently resigned from her position. The election is significant as it reflects the political dynamics in the district and the influence of national figures, including the president, on local candidates. The outcome could impact the balance of power in Congress and the future direction of the district's representation.

items.title: Georgia voters to pick replacement for Marjorie Taylor Greene in special election
items.description: The shadow of Greene, who resigned from Congress after a rift with the president, looms large over a crowded contest.

items.title: Voters head to the polls for a special election in Marjorie Taylor Greene's Georgia district
items.description: The Georgia district that sent Marjorie Taylor Greene, once a fierce defender of Donald Trump, to Congress is now deciding how to replace her — and whether to let the president make the pick

clusters.title: Haiti Drone Strikes Result in Over 1,200 Deaths, Reports Indicate
clusters.summary: Drone strikes conducted by Haitian forces have reportedly resulted in over 1,200 deaths, including at least 60 civilians, particularly around Port-au-Prince. Human Rights Watch has raised concerns about the high civilian toll and the implications for human rights in the region. The ongoing violence highlights the urgent need for accountability and protection of civilians amidst escalating conflict.

items.title: Hundreds killed in Haiti drone strikes, including 60 civilians: Report
items.description: Human Rights Watch says drone strikes by Haitian forces kill more than 1,200 people in, near Port-au-Prince since 2025.

items.title: Hundreds killed in Haiti drone strikes, including 60 civilians, Human Rights Watch says - Reuters
items.description: Hundreds killed in Haiti drone strikes, including 60 civilians, Human Rights Watch says  Reuters

clusters.title: Volkswagen Projects Halved Profit by 2025 Amid Tariffs and Competition
clusters.summary: Volkswagen has announced a challenging outlook for 2025, projecting that its profit will be halved due to increasing tariffs and intensified competition in the Chinese market. The company is grappling with external pressures that could significantly impact its financial performance in the coming years. This situation highlights the broader challenges facing the automotive industry as it navigates geopolitical and economic shifts.

items.title: Volkswagen flags a tough year ahead as 2025 profit halves on tariffs, China competition - CNBC
items.description: Volkswagen flags a tough year ahead as 2025 profit halves on tariffs, China competition  CNBC

items.title: Volkswagen stung by tariffs, China battle as profit halves - Reuters
items.description: Volkswagen stung by tariffs, China battle as profit halves  Reuters

clusters.title: Investigators Search Epstein's Former Ranch in New Mexico
clusters.summary: Investigators in New Mexico have begun a search of Jeffrey Epstein's former ranch, known as Zorro Ranch, following the release of investigative files that have renewed scrutiny of the convicted sex offender's activities. The search aims to uncover any evidence related to Epstein's alleged crimes and the operations conducted at the property. This development highlights ongoing efforts to address the legacy of Epstein's actions and the implications for his associates.

items.title: US investigators begin search of Epstein’s former ranch in New Mexico
items.description: Search comes after the release of investigative files renewed attention to sex offender's activities at the Zorro Ranch.

items.title: New Mexico investigators search Epstein's former ranch - Reuters
items.description: New Mexico investigators search Epstein's former ranch  Reuters

clusters.title: Balendra Shah Poised to Become Nepal's Next Prime Minister
clusters.summary: Balendra Shah, a former rapper and leader of the Rastriya Swatantra party, is set to become Nepal's next prime minister following a significant electoral victory. His party's win comes in the wake of widespread protests led by the Gen Z movement, which aimed to bring about political change in the country. This election marks a pivotal moment in Nepal's political landscape, reflecting the influence of younger voters.

items.title: Who Is Balendra Shah, the Rapper Set to Be Nepal’s Next Prime Minister?
items.description: Balendra Shah’s party won a landslide in the election that followed Nepal’s Gen Z revolution. His style is pugnacious.

items.title: Ex-rapper Balendra Shah set to be Nepal PM after party’s landslide election win
items.description:
Shah’s Rastriya Swatantra party secures thumping victory in first poll since gen Z protests that toppled government

Balendra Shah, the rapper turned politician and popular figurehead of a gen Z revolution, looks set to become Nepal’s next prime minister after his party won by an unprecedented margin.

Shah, known widely as Balen, and his Rastriya Swatantra party (RSP) secured a rare landslide victory in the first election since youth-led protests during which dozens were killed and the former government was toppled.

Continue reading...

clusters.title: Justices Jackson and Kavanaugh Clash Over Trump Emergency Orders
clusters.summary: Supreme Court Justices Ketanji Brown Jackson and Brett Kavanaugh engaged in a public debate regarding the court's approach to emergency requests related to former President Donald Trump's agenda. Their differing perspectives highlight ongoing tensions within the court about the handling of such cases, which have significant implications for executive power and judicial authority. This exchange underscores the broader legal and political challenges surrounding Trump's initiatives.

items.title: Two Supreme Court Justices Debate Handling of Trump Emergency Cases
items.description: In a rare joint appearance, Justices Ketanji Brown Jackson and Brett M. Kavanaugh offered sharply different views on how the court should handle emergency requests.

items.title: Justices Jackson and Kavanaugh spar over Supreme Court orders favoring Trump
items.description: Supreme Court Justices Ketanji Brown Jackson and Brett Kavanaugh sparred over the many emergency orders the court has issued allowing President Donald Trump to move ahead with key parts of his agenda

clusters.title: US and South Korea Conduct Military Drills Amid Middle East Tensions
clusters.summary: The United States has commenced large-scale military exercises with South Korea, highlighting their ongoing defense collaboration. South Korea has indicated that the redeployment of US military assets to the Middle East could enhance its ability to deter regional threats. This development occurs against the backdrop of heightened tensions in the Middle East, raising questions about military resource allocation and strategic priorities.

clusters.summary: U.S. stock markets experienced gains while oil prices declined as tensions between the U.S. and Iran escalated, with President Trump making statements that shifted the tone of the conflict. This market reaction reflects investor sentiment amid geopolitical uncertainties, highlighting the interconnectedness of political events and economic indicators. The situation remains fluid, with potential implications for global markets and energy prices.

clusters.title: Timothée Chalamet Faces Backlash Over Ballet and Opera Remarks
clusters.summary: Actor Timothée Chalamet has sparked a viral backlash following comments he made about ballet and opera, which some critics argue reflect elitism. Supporters contend that the reaction to his remarks underscores the very issues he was addressing regarding cultural appreciation. The incident highlights ongoing debates about art forms and accessibility in contemporary society.

clusters.title: Justice Department Settles Monopoly Case with Live Nation
clusters.summary: The Justice Department has reached a settlement with Live Nation over allegations of illegal monopoly practices. However, a bipartisan group of states has opted not to sign the settlement, expressing concerns about its implications for competition in the live entertainment industry. This development highlights ongoing tensions regarding market control and regulatory oversight in the sector.

clusters.summary: Former President Donald Trump asserted that Iran has the capability to strike a girls' school, referencing their access to Tomahawk missiles. The claim has sparked debate regarding the accuracy and implications of such assertions in the context of U.S.-Iran relations.

clusters.title: Hims & Hers Partners with Novo Nordisk, Stops Selling Compounded GLP-1s
clusters.summary: Hims & Hers has announced a partnership with Novo Nordisk, leading to the cessation of its sales of compounded GLP-1 medications. This decision follows Novo Nordisk's withdrawal of a patent infringement lawsuit against the company regarding these weight loss drugs. The collaboration is expected to impact the market for obesity treatments and reflects a shift in Hims & Hers' business strategy.

clusters.title: Trump Administration Subpoenas Arizona 2020 Voting Records
clusters.summary: The Trump administration has issued a grand jury subpoena for voting records from the 2020 election in Arizona, a move that underscores ongoing efforts to investigate election integrity. This action has drawn attention from both supporters and critics, highlighting the contentious nature of election-related inquiries in the U.S. The implications of this subpoena may influence future discussions on voting laws and election oversight.

clusters.title: Trump Considers Control of Strait of Hormuz Amid Tensions
clusters.summary: Former President Donald Trump has indicated that he is contemplating the possibility of taking control of the Strait of Hormuz, a critical waterway for global oil shipments. This statement comes as he describes the ongoing conflict with Iran as 'very complete,' highlighting the escalating tensions in the region. The implications of such a move could significantly affect international trade and geopolitical stability.

clusters.summary: Authorities are investigating explosions that occurred near a synagogue in Belgium and outside the U.S. Embassy in Oslo, Norway. The investigations are ongoing as officials work to determine the circumstances surrounding the events.
